Authors.value: K. Fizazi, J.M. Piulats, M.N. Reaume, P. Ostler, R. McDermott, J.R. Gingerich, E. Pintus, S.S. Sridhar, R.M. Bambury, U. Emmenegger, H. Lindberg, D. Morris, F. Nolè, J. Staffurth, C. Redfern, M.I. Sáez, W. Abida, G. Daugaard, A. Heidenreich, L. Krieger, B. Sautois, A. Loehr, D. Despain, C.A. Heyes, S.P. Watkins, S. Chowdhury, C.J. Ryan, and A.H. Bryce

Full Pub or Abstract.value: Rucaparib or Physician's Choice in Metastatic Prostate Cancer. N Engl J Med 2023;388:719-32. DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa2214676

Control Arm.value: Physician's choice: docetaxel or second-generation ARPI (abiraterone acetate or enzalutamide)

Line of Therapy - mCRPC.value: Post-ARPI, no prior chemotherapy for mCRPC

Quality of Life Scale.value: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy–Prostate, Brief Pain Inventory–Short Form, EQ-5D-5L

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Imaging-based progression-free survival (PFS) according to independent review

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival, objective response according to independent imaging-based review

Type of Therapy.value: PARP inhibitor vs. physician's choice (docetaxel or second-generation ARPI)

Prior Therapy in Any Setting.value: Second-generation ARPI, taxane-based chemotherapy for castration-sensitive disease allowed

Prior Therapy in mCRPC Setting.value: Second-generation ARPI, no prior chemotherapy for mCRPC
